examples.title: Pipeline Execution Time Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate execution time for pipelined processor
examples.code:
CALCULATE_PIPELINE_TIME(n, k, avgStalls, clockTime)
// n = number of instructions
// k = number of pipeline stages
// avgStalls = average stalls per instruction
// clockTime = clock cycle time in ns

BEGIN
 baseCycles = k + (n - 1) // Fill pipeline + drain
 stallCycles = n * avgStalls // Total stall cycles
 totalCycles = baseCycles + stallCycles
 
 executionTime = totalCycles * clockTime
 
 speedup = (n * k * clockTime) / executionTime
 avgCPI = totalCycles / n
 
 RETURN executionTime, speedup, avgCPI
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates total execution time by combining base pipeline cycles (k + n - 1 for fill and drain) with stall cycles. Speedup is compared against non-pipelined execution time (n × k × clockTime). Average CPI includes both ideal cycles and stalls.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only uses a few variables

examples.title: Data Forwarding Logic
examples.description: Hardware logic to handle data hazards using forwarding paths
examples.explanation: Data forwarding routes results directly from pipeline registers to dependent instructions. ForwardA checks EX/MEM register first (most recent result), then MEM/WB register. This eliminates 2-cycle stalls for ALU-ALU dependencies.

examples.title: Branch Penalty Calculation
examples.description: Calculate effective CPI with branch prediction
examples.explanation: This calculates how branch mispredictions affect overall CPI. The formula multiplies branch frequency by misprediction rate by penalty to get average branch stalls per instruction. Adding to ideal CPI gives effective CPI.

examples.title: Pipeline Stall Insertion
examples.description: Algorithm to insert stalls when hazards cannot be resolved
examples.code:
DETECT_AND_STALL(IR, pipelineRegisters, hazardDetectUnit)
// Insert NOP (bubble) when hazard detected

BEGIN
 // Check for RAW hazard in ID stage
 if (hazardDetectUnit.detectRAW(ID/EX, IF/ID)) then
 // Stall: prevent IF/ID from updating
 IF/ID.writeEnable = FALSE
 // Insert bubble in ID/EX
 ID/EX = NOP_instruction
 // Allow previous instruction to proceed
 PC.writeEnable = FALSE
 cyclesStalled = 1
 
 // Check for load-use hazard (special RAW case)
 if (ID/EX.isLoad AND hazardDetectUnit.isDependency(ID/EX.dest, IF/ID.src)) then
 // Need 1 stall cycle for load data
 insertBubble(1)
 cyclesStalled = 1
 
 RETURN cyclesStalled
END

insertBubble(n)
BEGIN
 for i = 1 to n do
 Insert NOP instruction into pipeline
 Reduce performance
 end for
END
examples.explanation: Stall insertion prevents incorrect execution by freezing pipeline registers when hazards are detected. A bubble (NOP) is inserted to give time for dependency resolution. This guarantees correctness but reduces performance.
